violations.text: Cold- and/or hot-holding units (1 freezer) were observed in poor repair in the kitchen, nonfunctional during inspection. As noted during last inspection. PIC stated all kitchen power goes out when the unit is turned on. Unit must be removed or replaced. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/06/2025

Italian sub sandwiches observed held at 57 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC stated sandwiches had been made approximately 15 minutes earlier. Sandwiches were placed on sheet pans in a single layer in the freezer. Discussed maintaining cold temperatures throughout the production process, using the food thermometers frequently, and utilizing cold packs. New Violation. Correct By: 09/22/2025

One freezer was not in good repair, nonfunctional during inspection. PIC stated all kitchen power goes out when the unit is turned on. Unit must be removed or replaced. New Violation. Correct By: 10/06/2025
The non-food contact surfaces of the utensil drawers handles, and the can opener base have an accumulation of food residue, and other debris. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/22/2025
Gaskets on the two large milk coolers located in the cafeteria were not in good repair. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 11/07/2023
Leak was observed at the prep sink base at the time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 05/09/2024

Employees' clothing or other personal belongings were observed stored hanging on shelving that stores single use items and crackers in the storage area. Lockers or other suitable facilities shall be used for the orderly storage of employees' clothing and other possessions. Employees moved belongings to designated area. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/25/2024
The gaskets in the milk cooler in the cafeteria is in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 11/07/2023
